content: I haven't even touched it
<:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541> you can do it!
nor side story
well by events I mean map tile event dialogs
that's honestly what I ended up doing for 1.5 chapters worth of 5+6 and man it is not an easy task but I believe in you!
you can take your time though, there's no rush on anything and I don't think anyone will be upset if you take several months to work through it
The moment I finished ch 1-3, then I saw.... more stages to go...
if it helps, now that I'm done rewriting the Event dialogs I can probably upload that whole chunk as a base to work from...!
Ngl, your excel files helps a lot on the WOD

Maybe that's the solution to my problems
Should I quit obsessing about trying to make all of the english gameplay shit perfect, and just lean into my natural inclination to organize things to make it easier for everyone else to do their thing...?

just my personal thoughts on the matter, but I think a chunk of work that will take some manhours to do but would have a pretty significant impact on consistency/quality would be...

1. make a list of every single character currently in the game
2. go through each of them and pull their card lines into a sheet in order so you have an organized foundation of "needs-to-be-fixed" text like what you did for wod/relic
3. go through and just standardize the very base terms without touching anything else as a first pass (i.e., change all "obtain" into gain, change all "heal/recover/restore" into whatever term you choose to use, change all "apply/inflict/etc" into whatever term you choose to use
that will create a baseline foundation of fully consistent terminology that will make every basic card effect read the same to players
and then we can go through and work on the silly messed up stuff afterwards (like the "enjoys x% str" and "benefits from x% tentacle damage" and whatnot)
+1 vote from meh

honestly now that I understand how Git & GitHub works I'm pretty sure that's the solution to this entire shitshow lmao

Instead of making Excel sheets and going *hey look guys I helped* I should effectively chunk out and reupload the source files into organized xlsx files in my own repository, and have us all work within *that*
and then we can upload each of those files as an update to the main repo whenever there's significant progress
I'm pretty much done with steps 1 & 2, I'm just missing some things like tooltips, states, affixes etc. that I have to go find.
That's what I'm gonna do then. I'm gonna shift my focus from here on to lightening the organizational load off of Ace's shoulders now that I'm a mod.
the only thing I'm kind of worried about currently
is how often we're going to get updated source sheets (if ever)
yeah...
because we are (as a group) submitting a lot of changes
and anyone working off an outdated sheet is going to accidentally end up overwriting a lot of very hard work done by other people
that and honestly not having access to anything from like the past 1-2 months of updates in a game with regular content updates is kind of a problem
since character changes happen so much that I'm scared to try and work on characters in case someone got buffed and I didn't notice and I change the text
ven the existing text clearly has an issue with it because people have raised various issues where changes get reverted

i kinda see no issues for ID TL, because the general idea is still the same or at least similar.

or maybe that's just because I am still working on the earliest content

like the aequor realm mechanics buff where surging gets +1 tentacle per turn, that change was added to the tooltip after it was released but apparently at some point it was reverted (likely because someone merged an older version of the file, I can only assume) and right now ingame it's missing that text
same thing where like, the chaos mastery info for caro realm is wrong, or pandia's exalt info is redundant
this wheel for example has the wrong effect on it and we have no way to fix it
yeah, like I don't know what happened here between last update and this one, but whatever's going on it must be a chaotic nightmare inside biav
(and no I don't mean the broken tags, I meant the wheel effect itself is literally wrong and doesn't match the ingame implementation)
oh yeah, during the MTL pickman patch while Ace was hospitalized, Kath's card text all got reverted back to the base MTL text instead of the human translated text
oof...
which then got fixed when Ace pushed the human translation of Pickman's text but I think she missed one and it's still broken in kath's inbattle status
so there's definitely some very obvious versioning issues

but yeah that's what worries me because if BIAV is already having versioning problems, that is just going to get far more compounded once the new professional translators start work and their text gets implemented and we're still working on an outdated version
and just the delay in communication since no dev point of contact makes it kind of difficult to see what has/hasn't gotten changed in the various updates it'll take before it hits live
but I'm mostly just griping, Ace is doing a tremendous amount of work solo as the project manager and it's not really anything she can fix I don't think
in the end I think I might just be a little too impatient which is a personal failing on my part and not anyone else's problem to deal with
I figure, yeah. I *really* hope the new hire lifts some of that burden off Ace's shoulders.
the timeline for this is definitely in the multi-month range and I'm just too excited/passionate about wanting to fix things that I want the timescale to be on a days/weeks range
yeah... same 😅
it's okay though! I'm gonna do my best to help.
okay would it be *too* ironic to run these headers through GTL? 😂
I think those are just the default discord categories that happen to be in chinese because clientside language settings when creating the server
lol oh no
should be info, text channels, voice channels?

<@183782216353054720> double checked my uploads and they _should_ be correct! (attached img for confirmation) Special characters are readable to me on libreoffice
I believe because I work on google sheets it automatically exports UTF-8 as standard
which file is this?

this is EN_SalvadorAwakenerStory_Proofread in the img
I've also checked EN_GameplayPanelText_Proofread and EN_SalvadorSpecialOps_Proofread
ofc final confirmation from Ace would be perfect but they're working okay on my end
porting this convo from <#1402538040253087766> over here cus Adelle kinda had a point there
just me up to my BS again, yapping about verbs
accrue sounds weird to me
it's a thesaurus-sounding word
like a "nobody actually says this but someone went digging through the thesaurus just for the sake of finding a different word to use" kind of thing
aw shit I've been caught thesaurus-handed
buuuut again I have always been in favor of using simple terms that clearly communicate effects for standard gameplay
gain/grant/recover/heal/inflict are great words that do lots of great work
also on further thought considering Ace already told us not to make any sweeping changes, shouldn't we just be sticking with what's established ingame currently
I feel like the debate shouldn't be "what word do we want to use for x" and more "the game uses currently either obtain or gain for aliemus, which one do we standardize on"

I'm neutral on a lot of this and could go most directions if enough consensus could be shown.
I 100% agree with your points here Lens, *and* have a counterpoint;
You know what *also* is a "nobody actually says this but someone went digging through the thesaurus just for the sake of finding a different word to use" kind of thing?
*literally all of the stupid shit lovecraft ever penned*
ye which is why i agree with thesaurus digging for story/lore
but a game should be a game first and foremost so i think we should keep gameplay and story separate
...god I want that worldbuilding document

your point about the massive crazy changes that might be coming down the pipeline for basically everything like how they're doing it in the JP servers (which is insane, I had no idea) honestly puts a LOT of weight behind your constant reminders that my whole "make the entire game read gud" project is ultimately, *ironically*, short-sighted.

I really hope they loop me in on helping do this whole thing when they rework all this shit later down the line, but for now honestly you're right and I gotta accept that

I thought I could come on and just "fix" everything, which by all means technically I totally can, but the fact that when they said they want to redo the CN gameplay stuff they *could* mean that they want to completely restructure kits and how certain game mechanics work, means that any work I do on perfecting the current state of the game might legitimately be wasted...
definitely not me crashing tf out, I'm ok
I did warn you!!
that's why I kept saying you should watch out because without lockstep dev communications it's a doomed venture

it's just difficult for me to do work I'm passionate about and *intentionally* do a "just good enough" job.
But if it's what Ace wants, I'll set my sights lower and get what needs to be done actually done first

I do feel it, a big reason on why I slowed down on the story translations was because I initially wanted to do a quick fast-pass for grammar/consistency/major glaring TL issues and not a detailed dive
and then like two chapters in i realized i was getting through maybe 30-40 lines an hour because I was effectively retranslating it from scratch and rewriting every line
and I was like oh...I should stop <:karen_nervous:1408324291237511259>
(and see now we have the professional work starting on it so most of that effort was wasted anyway! but I already knew that was coming so I have nobody to blame but myself)
lol
I really should have just powered through all of a1 in a week or so like my original intent but man the quality would have been so bad that it doesn't even feel like it would've been worth doing
on the flip side I am *very* proud of ch5 and I'm hoping the half of ch6 I did do gets sent to the professionals because I think it was done very well

I gotta check with Sophie at some point on miryam story progress since if she hasn't gotten too far into it I might just start working on that as my next big thing because I think just a proofreading isn't gonna cut it
im crying tears of blood from solo TL-ing almost every single stuff... hwe....

idk, it's just really hard for me to do anything in the gameplay department without it technically classifying as "sweeping changes"
like, to even fix the issues that are "in need of fixing", I need to have a consistent set of terms to use, and implementing that consistency means changing a little bit of *everything*
even just the ch4/5/6 stuff I did had an immense amount of mistranslations which changed a lot of meanings which I think are going to be hard to catch if you can't understand the original CN
and like... I did it with the Events, so it's not impossible
now that you mentioned events
the events looked good to me (though admittedly I only skimmed through it)
